serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of broccoli stir-fried with onion and mushroom as served at a Singaporean Zi Char stall typically weighs approximately 300 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (HPB Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Broccoli Stir-Fried with Onion and Mushroom' exists in the HPB Singapore nutrient database. Values are estimated by compositing HPB entries for cooked broccoli, cooked mushroom, and cooked onion with typical Zi Char stir-fry sauce proportions (oyster sauce, soy sauce, vegetable oil). The input record's per_100g values (energy 93.33 kcal, protein 3.33 g, fat 6.0 g, carbohydrate 6.67 g) are consistent with this composite estimation and are retained. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for broccoli (ID 11090), mushrooms (ID 11260), and onion (ID 11282) were used as cross-reference validation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite stir-fried broccoli dish. GI estimated at 15 based on the analogous food 'broccoli, cooked' (GI ~15, low-GI vegetable) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Mushrooms and onions are also low-GI foods. The oyster sauce and cornstarch thickener contribute a small amount of refined carbohydrate but the overall dish carbohydrate content is low (6.67 g/100g), keeping the glycemic load per 300 g serving at approximately 3.0 (6.67 g carbohydrate × 0.15 GI factor × 3 = ~3.0 GL units), which is low.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 400 mg per 100 g from soy sauce and oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: At 400 mg sodium per 100 g, a standard 300 g plate delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium — over half the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg — making this dish unsuitable for hypertensive patients without explicit modification to use low-sodium soy sauce, reduced oyster sauce, and no added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (6.67 g/100 g), low GI (~15), high dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content, low estimated GI of approximately 15, and 2.0 g dietary fibre per 100 g support moderate glycaemic control; patients should request minimal cornstarch thickening and reduced oyster sauce to limit added refined carbohydrate and sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (400 mg/100 g) offset by low saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g), zero trans fat, and cardioprotective phytonutrients from broccoli and mushrooms
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g), zero trans fat, and cardioprotective sulforaphane from broccoli are favourable for cardiovascular health, but the high sodium content (400 mg/100 g) poses a significant risk for blood pressure management and should be reduced by requesting low-sodium soy sauce and minimal oyster sauce.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory sulforaphane from broccoli, beta-glucans from mushrooms, vitamin C 33.33 mg/100 g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Broccoli's sulforaphane and mushrooms' beta-glucans provide anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating benefits relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; however, the high sodium (400 mg/100 g) can exacerbate tissue oedema and should be minimised by requesting reduced soy sauce and oyster sauce.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Broccoli Stir-Fried with Onion and Mushroom is a classic Singaporean Zi Char vegetable stir-fry featuring fresh broccoli florets, sliced onions, and earthy mushrooms (typically button or shiitake) cooked over high heat in a wok with garlic, oyster sauce, soy sauce, and a small amount of vegetable oil, often finished with a light cornstarch slurry to create a glossy savoury sauce. The dish has a tender-crisp texture with a umami-rich, mildly sweet and savoury flavour profile. It is a staple side dish at Zi Char (煮炒) stalls across Singapore and Malaysia, typically ordered as part of a shared meal alongside rice and other dishes. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 93 kcal, 3.3 g protein, 6.0 g fat (of which only 1.0 g is saturated), 6.7 g carbohydrate, 2.0 g dietary fibre, and 33.3 mg vitamin C. The sodium content is elevated at 400 mg per 100 g due to soy sauce and oyster sauce, making a standard 300 g plate deliver approximately 1200 mg sodium. The dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low GI (~15) and low carbohydrate content, but requires sodium reduction for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. Broccoli contributes sulforaphane, a cruciferous anti-inflammatory compound, and mushrooms contribute beta-glucans with immune-modulating properties.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this composite dish exists in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing approved-source data for the individual ingredients (broccoli, mushroom, onion) with typical Zi Char stir-fry sauce proportions. The input record's values are consistent with this estimation and are retained. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact sauce proportions and oil quantity used in any given Zi Char preparation are variable.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite stir-fried vegetable dish is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2024. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for this specific composite dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore database; estimated potassium from broccoli alone would be approximately 270 mg/100 g but this is not confirmed for the composite dish. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not available for this specific composite dish in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible trans fat in this vegetable stir-fry preparation using standard vegetable oil; no hydrogenated fats are used. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on cooked broccoli GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. The input record listed primary_cuisine as 'Malaysian' but the dish description, data source (GrabFood Singapore), and Zi Char context indicate Singaporean origin; cuisine has been corrected to 'Singaporean Zi Char' with country_of_origin Singapore. The input source URL https://foodo.sg/ is the HPB Foodo portal and is retained as the primary nutrient source reference. Q8 self-check (iteration 3): all strings in aliases_by_language verified verbatim against flat aliases array — english: 7 entries all present; chinese: 2 entries all present; malay: 1 entry present; tamil: 1 entry present; hindi: 1 entry present; romanized: 1 entry present. Total flat aliases: 13. No discrepancies found.
